fatigue.title: Your brain needs a reset
messages: You've been in deep focus for a while — your cognitive reserves are depleting. A 5-minute break now will buy you 45 more minutes of quality work.
Your signal patterns suggest mental fatigue. Step away from the screen. Walk to the window. Let your visual focus relax.
Sustained attention has limits. You've reached yours for this cycle. A short break isn't losing time — it's investing in the next hour.
Your typing rhythm and response patterns indicate fatigue. Close your eyes for 60 seconds. It genuinely helps.
FLOW is detecting a natural cognitive dip. This happens to everyone. A 5-minute walk will restore more than another 30 minutes of grinding will produce.

stuck.title: You've been circling the same problem
messages: You've spent significant time on this without progress. The answer is probably not where you've been looking. Try explaining the problem out loud.
Being stuck means your current mental model is wrong, not that you're not smart enough. Step back and ask: what am I assuming that might be false?
When you're stuck, the instinct is to try harder. The data says try differently. Break the problem into the smallest possible pieces.
You've been here a while. This is a signal to change strategy, not double down on the current one. Ask for help, rubber duck it, or switch tasks for 10 minutes.
FLOW sees you looping. The fastest path forward is usually not through — it's around. Consider approaching from a completely different angle.

passive.title: You've drifted from your intention
messages: You declared an intention when you started this session. The app you're using right now isn't it. Still worth doing?
You've been in passive consumption mode for a while. Your declared task is waiting. Even 10 focused minutes is worth more than this.
Context drift is normal. Noticing it is the skill. Your session goal was specific — want to get back to it?
Your attention has wandered. That's human. FLOW's job is just to surface it. What do you actually want to do with the next 20 minutes?
You started this session with a clear goal. You're currently not working toward it. Deliberate choice or unnoticed drift?

ultradian_break.title: Natural break point reached
messages: Your personal rhythm suggests this is a natural transition point. Your brain is ready for a short reset before the next cycle.
You've completed a full ultradian focus cycle. This is the ideal moment for a 10-15 minute break — not too early, not too late.
FLOW has learned your personal cycle length. This is your natural break point. Taking it now will improve the quality of the next cycle.
Your brain works in cycles. You just finished one. A short break here isn't weakness — it's working with your biology, not against it.
Peak cognitive performance comes from cycling between focus and recovery. Your recovery window is now.

meeting_warning.title: Meeting approaching
messages: You have a meeting in less than 30 minutes. Consider wrapping up your current train of thought and leaving a note for where to resume.
Calendar alert: a meeting is coming up. Deep work requires long uninterrupted blocks. You may not have enough time to go deep — consider lighter tasks now.
Upcoming meeting detected. If you start a deep focus session now, the meeting will interrupt your flow at the worst time. Plan accordingly.
You have a commitment coming up soon. This is a good time for shallow work, email, or review — tasks that don't require deep context.
Meeting in under 30 minutes. FLOW recommends not starting a new deep work session. Use this time for preparation or review instead.

deep_work.title: You are in deep flow
messages: Your signals are aligned. You're in deep work. FLOW will stay quiet and protect this state.
All signals nominal. Focus score high. Cognitive alignment confirmed. Keep going.
You're in the zone. FLOW sees it. No interruptions until your pattern says otherwise.
Strong behavioral signal, low drift, consistent rhythm. This is what peak cognitive work looks like. Don't stop.
Deep focus confirmed. FLOW is running silently in the background. You won't hear from us unless something changes.
